full_name: AIS-156 - Specific Requirements for L Category Electric Power Train Vehicles, Part I: Electrical Safety, Part II: REESS Safety
publisher: Automotive Industry Standards Committee (AISC), India / ARAI
ocr_quality: 良好（PDF 直接提取，英文內容完整）

conditions.passenger_compartment: Live parts inside passenger or luggage compartment shall meet IPXXD.
conditions.other_areas_with_passenger_compartment: Live parts in other accessible areas shall meet IPXXB.

criteria.description: Protection degree against direct contact shall satisfy the required IP probe condition for the installation zone.

tests.original_text_snippet: 5.1.1.1 For protection of live parts inside the passenger compartment or luggage compartment, the protection degree IPXXD shall be provided. 5.1.1.2.1 For vehicles with a passenger compartment, the protection degree IPXXB shall be satisfied.

tests.test_name_zh: 等電位/接地連續性要求

conditions.measurement: Resistance between all exposed conductive parts and the electrical chassis

criteria.description: Resistance between all exposed conductive parts and the electrical chassis shall be lower than 0.1 Ω.

exemptions: Requirement is satisfied if galvanic connection has been established by welding.
tests.original_text_snippet: 5.1.2.2 The resistance between all exposed conductive parts and the electrical chassis shall be lower than 0.1 Ω when there is current flow of at least 0.2 A.

conditions.dc_bus_requirement: 100 Ω/V of working voltage for DC buses
conditions.ac_bus_requirement: 500 Ω/V of working voltage for AC buses
conditions.combined_ac_dc_requirement: 500 Ω/V unless all AC buses are protected by double insulation or mechanically robust protections, then 100 Ω/V
conditions.method: Vehicle-based isolation resistance measurement per Annex 5A

criteria.description: Isolation resistance shall meet the bus-specific minimum requirement in Ω/V.

exemptions: For combined AC/DC buses, minimum may reduce to 100 Ω/V if all AC buses are protected by specified robust measures.
tests.original_text_snippet: 5.1.3.1 If AC buses and DC buses are galvanically isolated from each other, the isolation resistance shall have a minimum value of 100 Ω/V for DC buses and 500 Ω/V for AC buses.

tests.test_name_zh: 車載絕緣監測系統確認
tests.test_name_en: On-board Isolation Resistance Monitoring System Confirmation

conditions.method: Insert a resistor that does not reduce isolation below the minimum required value between monitored terminal and electrical chassis.

criteria.description: The warning shall be activated when the fault resistor is inserted.

tests.original_text_snippet: Annex 6: Insert a resistor that does not cause the isolation resistance to drop below the minimum required value. The warning shall be activated.

tests.test_name_zh: 氫氣排放測試（正常充電）
tests.test_name_en: Hydrogen Emission Test During Normal Charge
tests.applicability: Open type traction batteries / REESS with hydrogen emission concern

conditions.vehicle_based: Vehicle soaked at 293 ± 2 K for 12-36 h after discharge, then charged in sealed chamber.
conditions.component_based: REESS conditioned with minimum 5 standard cycles, soaked at 293 ± 2 K for 12-36 h, then charged in sealed chamber.
conditions.normal_charge: Charge at constant power during t1, then overcharge at constant current during t2.

criteria.description: During a normal charge procedure, hydrogen emissions shall be below 125 g during 5 h, or below 25 × t2 g during t2.

exemptions: Closed chemical process systems, e.g. lithium-ion battery systems, are considered emission-free under normal operation if properly documented.
tests.original_text_snippet: 5.4.3 During a normal charge procedure in the conditions given in Annex 7, hydrogen emissions shall be below 125 g during 5 h, or below 25 x t2 g during t2.

tests.test_name_zh: 氫氣排放測試（充電器故障）
tests.test_name_en: Hydrogen Emission Test During Charger Failure
tests.applicability: Open type traction batteries / REESS with hydrogen emission concern

conditions.charge_failure_procedure: Charging at constant power during t'1, then charging at maximum current recommended by the manufacturer during 30 minutes.
conditions.sealed_chamber: Hydrogen concentration, temperature, and pressure measured in sealed chamber

criteria.description: During a charge carried out with charger failure, hydrogen emissions shall be below 42 g during 30 minutes.

exemptions: Closed chemical process systems, e.g. lithium-ion battery systems, are considered emission-free under normal operation if properly documented.
tests.original_text_snippet: 5.4.4 During a charge carried out by a charger presenting a failure (conditions given in Annex 7), hydrogen emissions shall be below 42 g during 30 minutes.

conditions.soc: Upper 50 per cent of the normal operating SOC range
conditions.waveform: Sinusoidal logarithmic sweep between 7 Hz and 200 Hz and back to 7 Hz in 15 minutes
conditions.duration: 12 cycles for a total of 3 h in the vertical mounting direction
conditions.acceleration_lt_12kg: 7-18 Hz: 10 m/s²; 18-~50 Hz ramp to 80 m/s²; 50-200 Hz: 80 m/s²
conditions.acceleration_ge_12kg: 7-18 Hz: 10 m/s²; 18-~25 Hz ramp to 20 m/s²; 25-200 Hz: 20 m/s²
conditions.post_test: Standard cycle followed by 1 h observation

criteria.description: No electrolyte leakage, rupture (HV REESS only), fire, or explosion. For high voltage REESS, post-test isolation resistance shall be at least 100 Ω/V.

exemptions: A manufacturer-specific vibration profile may be used if verified and agreed, but approval is then limited to a specific vehicle type.
tests.original_text_snippet: Annex 8A: The tested-device shall be subjected to a vibration having a sinusoidal waveform with a logarithmic sweep between 7 Hz and 200 Hz... total of 3 hours... Acceptance criteria in 6.2.2.

conditions.soc: Upper 50 per cent of the normal operating SOC range
conditions.temperature_high: 60 ± 2°C or higher if requested by the manufacturer
conditions.temperature_low: -40 ± 2°C or lower if requested by the manufacturer
conditions.dwell_time: At least 6 h at each extreme
conditions.post_test: Standard cycle followed by 1 h observation

criteria.description: No electrolyte leakage, rupture (HV REESS only), fire, or explosion. For high voltage REESS, post-test isolation resistance shall be at least 100 Ω/V.

tests.original_text_snippet: Annex 8B: The tested-device shall be stored for at least six hours at 60°C ±2°C followed by at least six hours at -40°C ±2°C... minimum 5 total cycles.

tests.test_name_zh: 可拆卸 REESS 跌落測試
tests.test_name_en: Mechanical Drop Test for Removable REESS

conditions.soc: At least 90 per cent of rated capacity
conditions.surface: Smooth horizontal concrete pad or equivalent hardness
conditions.post_test: Standard cycle followed by 1 h observation

criteria.description: No electrolyte leakage, rupture (HV REESS only), fire, or explosion. For high voltage REESS, post-test isolation resistance shall be at least 100 Ω/V.

tests.original_text_snippet: Annex 8C: Free fall of the removed REESS from a height of 1.0 m... dropped six times from different orientations... Acceptance criteria in 6.4.1.2.

tests.applicability: Vehicles with a centre and/or side stand

conditions.soc: Upper 50 per cent of the normal operating SOC range
conditions.shock_lt_12kg: Half-sine shock, 1500 m/s² peak acceleration, 6 ms pulse duration
conditions.shock_ge_12kg: Half-sine shock, 500 m/s² peak acceleration, 11 ms pulse duration
conditions.directions: 3 positive and 3 negative shocks in each of three mutually perpendicular mounting positions, total 18 shocks
conditions.post_test: Standard cycle followed by 1 h observation

criteria.description: No electrolyte leakage, rupture (HV REESS only), fire, or explosion. For high voltage REESS, post-test isolation resistance shall be at least 100 Ω/V.

exemptions: Applies only to vehicles with a centre and/or side stand.
tests.original_text_snippet: Annex 8D: The tested-device shall be subjected to a half-sine shock... 18 shocks total. Acceptance criteria in 6.4.2.2.

conditions.soc: Upper 50 per cent of the normal operating SOC range
conditions.vehicle_or_component: Vehicle-based or component-based at manufacturer’s choice
conditions.fuel_pan: Fuel pan extending 20-50 cm beyond DUT horizontal projection
conditions.phase_C: Indirect flame exposure for 60 seconds using screen, or optional continuation of phase B for 60 seconds
conditions.phase_D: Observe until DUT cools to ambient or has been decreasing for at least 3 h

criteria.description: During the test, the tested-device shall exhibit no evidence of explosion.

exemptions: Test required only for REESS containing flammable electrolyte.
Manufacturer may choose vehicle-based or component-based execution.
tests.original_text_snippet: 6.5.3.1 During the test, the tested-device shall exhibit no evidence of explosion.

conditions.ambient_temperature: 20 ± 10°C or higher if requested by manufacturer
conditions.soc: Upper 50 per cent of the normal operating SOC range
conditions.contactors: All relevant main contactors for charging and discharging closed
conditions.duration: Until protection interrupts/limits current, or at least 1 h after casing temperature stabilises (gradient <4°C through 1 h)
conditions.post_test: Standard cycle followed by 1 h observation

criteria.description: No electrolyte leakage, rupture (HV REESS only), fire, or explosion. For high voltage REESS, post-test isolation resistance shall be at least 100 Ω/V.

tests.original_text_snippet: Annex 8F: The positive and negative terminals shall be connected to produce a short circuit... resistance not exceeding 5 mΩ. Acceptance criteria in 6.6.2.

conditions.ambient_temperature: 20 ± 10°C or higher if requested by manufacturer
conditions.charge_current: At least 1/3 C rate but not exceeding the maximum current within the normal operating range specified by the manufacturer
conditions.contactors: All relevant main contactors for charging closed
conditions.end_condition: Until automatic interrupt/limit occurs, or until twice rated charge capacity if interrupt fails
conditions.post_test: Standard cycle followed by 1 h observation

criteria.description: No electrolyte leakage, rupture (HV REESS only), fire, or explosion. For high voltage REESS, post-test isolation resistance shall be at least 100 Ω/V.

tests.original_text_snippet: Annex 8G: The tested-device shall be charged with at least 1/3 C rate... charging continued until the tested-device interrupts or limits charging, or to twice rated charge capacity.

conditions.ambient_temperature: 20 ± 10°C or higher if requested by manufacturer
conditions.discharge_current: At least 1/3 C rate but not exceeding the maximum current within the normal operating range specified by the manufacturer
conditions.end_condition: Until tested-device interrupts/limits discharge, or until 25 per cent of nominal voltage level if interrupt fails
conditions.post_test: Standard charge followed by 1 h observation

criteria.description: No electrolyte leakage, rupture (HV REESS only), fire, or explosion. For high voltage REESS, post-test isolation resistance shall be at least 100 Ω/V.

tests.original_text_snippet: Annex 8H: A discharge shall be performed with at least 1/3 C rate... continued until the tested-device interrupts or limits the discharging, or until 25 per cent of nominal voltage level.

conditions.cooling_system: Deactivate cooling system if REESS remains functional without cooling
conditions.temperature_measurement: Continuous measurement inside casing near cells
conditions.operation: Continuously charge and discharge with a steady current that increases cell temperature as rapidly as possible within normal operation range
conditions.chamber_temp: Increase to the protective threshold temperature or maximum operational temperature, then maintain
end_conditions: temperature stabilised with gradient <4°C through 2 h

criteria.description: No electrolyte leakage, rupture (HV REESS only), fire, or explosion. For high voltage REESS, post-test isolation resistance shall be at least 100 Ω/V.

exemptions: If no specific protection measures are necessary, safe operation under these conditions must be demonstrated instead.
tests.original_text_snippet: Annex 8I: The tested-device shall be continuously charged and discharged... chamber temperature gradually increased... Acceptance criteria in 6.9.2.

conditions.test_voltage_ac: 2 × (Un + 1200) V rms at 50/60 Hz for 1 minute
conditions.test_voltage_dc_alternative: Equivalent peak-value DC voltage for 1 minute
conditions.measurement_after: After test, apply 500 V DC between all charger inputs and exposed conductive parts / electrical chassis

criteria.description: Insulation resistance shall be equal to or greater than 7 MΩ when applying 500 V DC.

exemptions: Applies to vehicles with on-board (built-in) charger.
tests.original_text_snippet: 5.1.2.4.1.2 Acceptance criteria: The insulation resistance shall be equal to or greater than 7 MΩ when applying 500 V DC between all the inputs connected together and the vehicle’s exposed conductive parts/electrical chassis.

conditions.distance: 2.5 m to 3 m from nozzle to enclosure surface
conditions.duration: 1 min per square metre, minimum 3 min
conditions.post_test: Apply 500 V DC between all high voltage inputs and exposed conductive parts / electrical chassis

criteria.description: After water resistance test, insulation resistance shall be equal to or greater than 7 MΩ when applying 500 V DC.

exemptions: Applies to vehicles with on-board (built-in) charger.
tests.original_text_snippet: 5.1.2.4.2.2 Acceptance Criteria: The insulation resistance shall be equal to or greater than 7 MΩ, when applying 500 V DC.

exemptions_summary.exemption: Closed chemical process systems, e.g. lithium-ion batteries, may be considered emission-free under normal operation if documented by the manufacturer.
Applies only to vehicles with a centre and/or side stand.
Required for REESS containing flammable electrolyte only; manufacturer may choose vehicle-based or component-based execution.
Applies to vehicles with on-board charger.

metadata.extraction_method: Manual reading and analysis of complete .md file (PDF extraction)
metadata.ocr_quality_notes: OCR 品質良好，使用 PDF 直接提取，英文內容完整。AIS-156 is the Indian L-category electric vehicle safety standard based on UN ECE R136. It covers vehicle electrical safety and REESS safety, including hydrogen emissions for open batteries, REESS environmental/mechanical abuse, and on-board charger dielectric/water resistance tests.
